# Republic Studio - GitHub Setup Guide\n\n**Turn GitHub into Republic Studio - the central hub for all Republic development.**\n\nThis guide walks through setting up GitHub as a complete project management system for Production Waves development.\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nRepublic Studio uses GitHub as the complete development platform:\n\n```\n                    REPUBLIC STUDIO\n                    (GitHub as Hub)\n                         │\n        ┌────────────────┼────────────────┐\n        │                │                │\n    📋 Issues          📊 Projects        💬 Discussions\n  (Task Mgmt)       (Sprint Boards)    (Strategy/Feedback)\n        │                │                │\n  • Epics            • Wave Boards      • Design Debates\n  • Features         • Active Sprint    • Feature Ideas\n  • Bugs             • Velocity         • Questions\n  • Tasks            • Milestones       • Announcements\n\n     📚 Documentation    💻 Source Code    ✅ CI/CD Pipeline\n     (Design Source)     (Implementation)   (Verification)\n          │                   │                  │\n     Design Docs         Game Systems       Automated Tests\n     Architecture        Features           Build Checks\n     Roadmap             AI Systems         Deployment\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part 1: Create Wave-Based Project Boards\n\n### Setup Instructions\n\n1. **Go to Repository**\n   - Navigate to: https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game\n   - Click the **Projects** tab\n   - Click **New Project**\n\n2. **Create 6 Wave Boards**\n\nCreate one project board per Production Wave:\n\n#### Board 1: Wave 0 - Foundation\n- **Title**: Wave 0 - Foundation\n- **Description**: Build nothing visible. Only architecture. (2-3 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Custom Fields**:\n  - Priority (High, Medium, Low)\n  - Story Points (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13)\n  - Assigned Dev\n  - Wave (Wave-0-Foundation)\n\n#### Board 2: Wave 1 - Simulation Core\n- **Title**: Wave 1 - Simulation Core\n- **Description**: Build the world without presentation. (3-4 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 3: Wave 2 - Decision Interface\n- **Title**: Wave 2 - Decision Interface\n- **Description**: Build how players make decisions. (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 4: Wave 3 - Emergence & Depth\n- **Title**: Wave 3 - Emergence & Depth\n- **Description**: Build interconnected consequences. (6-8 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 5: Wave 4 - Intelligence & Autonomy\n- **Title**: Wave 4 - Intelligence & Autonomy\n- **Description**: Build thinking players (AI). (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 6: Wave 5 - Multiplayer & Emergence\n- **Title**: Wave 5 - Multiplayer & Emergence\n- **Description**: Build multiplayer politics. (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 7: Wave 6 - Presentation & Polish\n- **Title**: Wave 6 - Presentation & Polish\n- **Description**: Build what you see. (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 8: Active Sprint (Master Board)\n- **Title**: Active Sprint - Republic Studio\n- **Description**: Real-time view of current work across all Waves\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Filters**: All open issues assigned to current sprint\n\n### Configure Each Board with Columns\n\nFor each Wave board, set up columns:\n\n```\n┌──────────────┬─────────────┬────────────┬──────────────┬────────┐\n│   Backlog    │  Not Started│ In Progress│ In Review    │  Done  │\n├──────────────┼─────────────┼────────────┼──────────────┼────────┤\n│ Not assigned │ Ready to    │ Actively   │ PR/testing   │ Ready  │\n│ to sprint    │ start, no   │ being      │ phase        │ for    │\n│              │ blockers    │ worked on  │              │ next   │\n│              │             │            │              │ wave   │\n└──────────────┴─────────────┴────────────┴──────────────┴────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part 2: Create GitHub Milestones\n\nMilestones represent **Wave Completion Gates**.\n\n1. Go to **Issues** → **Milestones** → **New Milestone**\n\n2. Create one milestone per Wave:\n\n```\nMilestone: Wave 0 - Foundation Complete\n- Description: Architecture complete, buildable project\n- Due Date: End of Week 3\n- Gate: \"The skeleton works\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 1 - Simulation Core Complete\n- Description: World simulation running independently\n- Due Date: End of Week 7\n- Gate: \"The world lives without a player\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 2 - Decision Interface Complete\n- Description: Player can see and influence world\n- Due Date: End of Week 13\n- Gate: \"The player has an office\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 3 - Emergence & Depth Complete\n- Description: Systems interconnected, consequences cascade\n- Due Date: End of Week 21\n- Gate: \"Every choice matters and echoes\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 4 - Intelligence & Autonomy Complete\n- Description: AI nations autonomous, world responsive\n- Due Date: End of Week 27\n- Gate: \"The world doesn't need you to survive\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 5 - Multiplayer & Emergence Complete\n- Description: Multiple players supported, politics personal\n- Due Date: End of Week 33\n- Gate: \"Politics becomes personal\"\n\nMilestone: Wave 6 - Presentation & Polish Complete\n- Description: Polished, beautiful, accessible, release ready\n- Due Date: End of Week 39\n- Gate: \"The world looks like leadership\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part 3: Create GitHub Labels\n\nLabels organize issues by Wave and category.\n\n1. Go to **Issues** → **Labels** → **New Label**\n\n2. Create Wave Labels:\n\n```\nWave Labels:\n- Wave-0-Foundation (color: #1f6feb)\n- Wave-1-Simulation (color: #0969da)\n- Wave-2-Interface (color: #1158c7)\n- Wave-3-Emergence (color: #033d8b)\n- Wave-4-Intelligence (color: #0a3069)\n- Wave-5-Multiplayer (color: #1f6feb)\n- Wave-6-Presentation (color: #0969da)\n\nPriority Labels:\n- Priority-Critical (color: #d1242f)\n- Priority-High (color: #fb8500)\n- Priority-Medium (color: #ffb703)\n- Priority-Low (color: #80b918)\n\nCategory Labels:\n- type-epic (color: #cf2e1f)\n- type-feature (color: #4abdac)\n- type-bug (color: #f7b731)\n- type-chore (color: #8ac926)\n- type-docs (color: #5762ff)\n\nStatus Labels:\n- status-blocked (color: #d1242f)\n- status-in-progress (color: #0969da)\n- status-ready-review (color: #6f42c1)\n- status-done (color: #28a745)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part 4: Link Issues to Waves and Boards\n\n### For Each Issue:\n\n1. **Add Wave Label** (determines which Wave it belongs to)\n   - Example: `Wave-0-Foundation`\n\n2. **Assign to Project Board**\n   - Click \"Projects\" in right sidebar\n   - Select corresponding Wave board\n   - Add to \"Backlog\" column\n\n3. **Set Priority**\n   - `Priority-Critical`, `Priority-High`, etc.\n\n4. **Set Milestone**\n   - Select corresponding Wave milestone\n   - Example: \"Wave 0 - Foundation Complete\"\n\n5. **Link to Design Docs**\n   - In description, add:\n   ```markdown\n   ## Design Reference\n   - Design Principles: [link]\n   - Feature Spec: [link]\n   - Architecture: [link]\n   ```\n\n---\n\n## Part 5: Set Up Issue Templates\n\nCreate consistent issue formats.\n\n1. Go to **Settings** → **Features** → **Issues**\n2. Enable Issue Templates\n3. Create templates for:\n   - Epic\n   - Feature\n   - Bug\n   - Technical Debt\n\n### Epic Template\n\n```markdown\n## Wave\n- [ ] Wave 0 - Foundation\n- [ ] Wave 1 - Simulation Core\n- [ ] Wave 2 - Decision Interface\n(etc)\n\n## Description\n[What is this epic about?]\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Criterion 1\n- [ ] Criterion 2\n\n## Republic Test\n- [ ] Strengthens executive leadership experience?\n- [ ] Creates meaningful strategic choices?\n- [ ] Affects multiple systems?\n- [ ] Fits office-centric design?\n- [ ] Increases replayability?\n- [ ] Enables emergent storytelling?\n- [ ] Reinforces player role as Head of Government?\n\n## Design Reference\n- Design Principles: [link]\n- Feature Spec: [link]\n```\n\n### Feature Template\n\n```markdown\n## Epic\n[Link to parent epic]\n\n## Wave\n- [ ] Wave 0 - Foundation\n- [ ] Wave 1 - Simulation Core\n(etc)\n\n## Description\n[What feature are we building?]\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Feature works as specified\n- [ ] Tests pass (90%+ coverage)\n- [ ] Documented\n- [ ] Code reviewed\n- [ ] No compiler warnings\n\n## Technical Notes\n[Any technical considerations]\n\n## Design Reference\n- Feature Spec: [link]\n- Architecture: [link]\n```\n\n---\n\n## Part 6: Daily Workflow\n\n### Morning Stand-up\n\n1. **Check Active Sprint Board**\n   - What's \"In Progress\"?\n   - What's \"In Review\"?\n   - Any blockers?\n\n2. **Pick Next Issue**\n   - From current Wave board\n   - Move to \"In Progress\"\n   - Assign to self\n\n### During Day\n\n1. **Update Issue Status**\n   - Completed? Move to \"In Review\"\n   - Stuck? Move to \"Blocked\" and comment\n   - Found new work? Create new issue, link to current\n\n2. **Create Pull Request**\n   - Link to issue (GitHub auto-closes)\n   - Reference design docs\n   - Request review\n\n3. **Move to Done**\n   - PR merged → Issue moves to Done (if automated)\n   - Manually move if needed\n   - Celebrate! 🎉\n\n### Weekly Sprint Planning\n\n1. **Review Current Wave Backlog**\n   - What's ready?\n   - What's blocked?\n   - What needs clarification?\n\n2. **Move to \"Not Started\"**\n   - Issues ready for this sprint\n   - Set priority and story points\n   - Assign developers\n\n3. **Review Velocity**\n   - Story points completed last sprint\n   - Trending up/down/stable?\n   - Adjust estimates\n\n### Wave Completion\n\n1. **Ensure All Issues \"Done\"**\n   - Run Wave project board\n   - Move remaining to next Wave\n   - Merge any stragglers\n\n2. **Achieve Wave Milestone**\n   - Mark milestone as complete\n   - Document completion in Docs\n   - Create Wave summary\n\n3. **Begin Next Wave**\n   - Open next Wave project board\n   - Move top priority issues to \"Not Started\"\n   - Start sprint planning\n\n---\n\n## Part 7: Automation Setup\n\n### Enable Auto-Draft Issues\n\nFor each Wave project board:\n1. Click **Settings** (gear icon)\n2. Go to **Automation**\n3. Enable:\n   - Auto-add new issues to Wave board\n   - Auto-move PRs to \"In Review\" when created\n   - Auto-move to \"Done\" when PR merged\n\n### GitHub Actions (CI/CD)\n\nCreate workflows for:\n- ✅ Run tests on PR\n- ✅ Check code coverage\n- ✅ Lint code\n- ✅ Build verification\n- ✅ Comment on PR with results\n\n---\n\n## Part 8: Reporting & Metrics\n\n### Velocity Tracking\n\n1. Each sprint, sum story points completed\n2. Plot on simple spreadsheet\n3. Look for trend (should stabilize)\n4. Use for future sprint planning\n\n### Wave Progress\n\n1. Count issues in Wave board\n2. % Complete = (Done issues / Total issues) × 100\n3. Track weekly\n4. Report to stakeholders\n\n### Burndown Chart\n\n1. Count remaining story points daily\n2. Plot on graph\n3. Should trend downward by sprint end\n4. Shows if sprint on-track\n\n---\n\n## Part 9: Success Checklist\n\n**Republic Studio is working when:**\n\n✅ All Wave work visible on appropriate board\n✅ Issues moved across columns as work progresses\n✅ Active Sprint board updated daily\n✅ Issues linked to design documentation\n✅ Velocity tracked and trending\n✅ Blockers identified in comments\n✅ Wave completion gates achieved on schedule\n✅ Team communicates via GitHub (issues, discussions, PRs)\n✅ Everything lives in GitHub (one source of truth)\n✅ Milestones mark Wave completion\n✅ Labels organize issues consistently\n✅ Project boards show real-time status\n\n---\n\n## Part 10: Links & References\n\n### Documentation\n- [Production Waves Strategy](./PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n- [Roadmap (by Wave)](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md)\n- [Features (by Wave)](../Docs/Features/README.md)\n- [Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)\n- [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md)\n\n### GitHub\n- [Republic-Game Repository](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game)\n- [Issues](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/issues)\n- [Projects](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/projects)\n- [Milestones](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/milestones)\n- [Discussions](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/discussions)\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. ✅ Create Wave project boards (Part 1)\n2. ✅ Create milestones (Part 2)\n3. ✅ Create labels (Part 3)\n4. ✅ Link existing issues to Waves (Part 4)\n5. ✅ Set up issue templates (Part 5)\n6. ✅ Start first sprint\n7. ✅ Track velocity\n8. ✅ Complete Wave 0 → celebrate! 🎉\n9. ✅ Begin Wave 1\n10. ✅ Repeat through Wave 6\n\n---\n\n*Republic Studio: Everything in GitHub. Production Waves drive development. One source of truth.*\n"
